project.name: PropManager Dashboard Communication System
project.description: World-class tenant-landlord communication infrastructure implementing WhatsApp-first messaging, real-time WebSocket updates via Laravel Reverb, and intelligent multi-channel notification routing for a Kenya-focused property management SaaS

qualityStandards.description: This is the communication backbone of PropManager. Every notification must be delivered reliably, every real-time update must be instant, and every message must reach the tenant through their preferred channel. In Kenya, WhatsApp is not a nice-to-have—it's the primary communication channel. Build for 99.9% delivery reliability.
principles: WhatsApp is the PRIMARY channel in Kenya/Africa markets (90%+ penetration)
Real-time updates for payment confirmations are non-negotiable—tenants expect instant feedback
Multi-channel fallback ensures no message is ever lost
Two-way communication captures tenant context and reduces support load
Rate limiting protects against spam and API cost overruns
All templates must be Meta-approved for WhatsApp Business compliance
Delivery status tracking for accountability and debugging
Quiet hours respect tenant preferences and local regulations

feedbackLoops.description: Run ALL feedback loops before committing. Do NOT commit if any fail.

commands.typecheck: npm run build -- --mode development 2>&1 | grep -i error
commands.test_websocket: php artisan reverb:start --debug & sleep 5 && curl -I http://localhost:8080

whatsapp_integration.description: WhatsApp Business API as primary communication channel with Meta-approved templates

realtime_broadcasting.description: Laravel Reverb WebSocket infrastructure for instant dashboard updates

notification_routing.description: Intelligent multi-channel routing with fallback chains and preference handling

two_way_communication.description: Inbound message handling from WhatsApp, capturing tenant responses as notes/tickets

dashboard_sync.description: Real-time synchronization between tenant and landlord dashboards

items.title: Set WhatsApp as Primary Notification Channel
items.description: Modify NotificationService to prioritize WhatsApp over email/SMS. WhatsApp should be the first attempted channel for all tenant communications when whatsapp_number is available.
steps: Audit NotificationService::send() method for current channel ordering
Implement channel priority logic: WhatsApp → SMS → Email → In-app
Add whatsapp_number validation (must be valid E.164 format: +254...)
Update NotificationPreference model to track whatsapp_opted_in boolean
Add migration for whatsapp_opted_in if not exists
Update frontend preference UI to show WhatsApp as primary option
Test with Twilio sandbox before production
items.verification: Send test notification to user with whatsapp_number set; verify WhatsApp is attempted first

items.title: Create Meta-Approved WhatsApp Message Templates
items.description: WhatsApp Business API requires pre-approved templates for business-initiated messages. Create and submit templates for all notification types to Meta for approval.
steps: Document all notification types that need WhatsApp templates
Create template content for each type with proper placeholders
Submit templates to Meta via Twilio Console for approval
Store approved template SIDs in config/whatsapp.php
Create TemplateService method to render WhatsApp templates
Add template validation before sending (ensure all placeholders filled)
Implement template fallback for unapproved messages (convert to session message)

templates.name: rent_reminder
templates.content: Hi {{1}}, your rent of KES {{2}} is due on {{3}}. Pay via M-Pesa to avoid late fees. Questions? Reply to this message.

templates.name: payment_received
templates.content: Thank you {{1}}! We received your payment of KES {{2}} (Ref: {{3}}). Your new balance is KES {{4}}.

templates.name: invoice_ready
templates.content: Hi {{1}}, your invoice {{2}} for KES {{3}} is ready. Due date: {{4}}. View: {{5}}

templates.name: arrears_notice
templates.content: Hi {{1}}, you have an outstanding balance of KES {{2}} that is {{3}} days overdue. Please settle to avoid penalties.

templates.name: maintenance_update
templates.content: Hi {{1}}, your maintenance request #{{2}} has been updated to: {{3}}. {{4}}

templates.name: lease_renewal
templates.content: Hi {{1}}, your lease expires on {{2}}. We'd like to offer renewal at KES {{3}}/month. Reply YES to proceed or contact us.

items.verification: Verify templates are approved in Twilio Console; send test message with each template

items.title: Implement WhatsApp Delivery Status Webhooks
items.description: Track WhatsApp message delivery status (sent, delivered, read, failed) via Twilio webhooks. Update notification records with status changes.
steps: Create WhatsApp webhook controller: WhatsAppWebhookController
Implement status callback endpoint: POST /webhooks/whatsapp/status
Map Twilio status events to notification statuses
Update Notification model with whatsapp_message_sid field
Implement status update logic with pessimistic locking
Add read receipt tracking (blue ticks)
Log all webhook events for debugging
Configure Twilio to send status callbacks to webhook URL
items.verification: Send WhatsApp message; verify status updates flow through webhook to database

items.title: Implement Multi-Channel Fallback Chain
items.description: When WhatsApp delivery fails or times out, automatically fallback to SMS, then email. Track which channel ultimately succeeded.
steps: Create FallbackNotificationJob that handles retries across channels
Implement delivery timeout detection (1 hour for WhatsApp → fallback)
Add fallback_channel field to notifications table
Create scheduled command to process stuck 'pending' WhatsApp messages
Implement exponential backoff for retries within same channel
Add notification to landlord when tenant unreachable on all channels
Track fallback statistics for analytics dashboard
items.verification: Block WhatsApp (invalid number); verify SMS sent after 1 hour timeout

items.title: Install and Configure Laravel Reverb
items.description: Set up Laravel Reverb as the WebSocket server for real-time broadcasting. This replaces the need for Pusher and runs self-hosted.
steps: Run: composer require laravel/reverb
Run: php artisan reverb:install
Configure .env with Reverb settings
Update config/broadcasting.php to use reverb driver
Create reverb.php config file if not auto-generated
Set up Supervisor to run reverb:start in production
Configure CORS for WebSocket connections
Test WebSocket connection from browser console
items.verification: Run php artisan reverb:start; connect from browser; see connection in Reverb logs

items.title: Install and Configure Laravel Echo Frontend
items.description: Set up Laravel Echo on the Vue 3 frontend to receive real-time broadcasts from Reverb.
steps: Run: npm install laravel-echo pusher-js
Create Echo configuration in resources/js/echo.js
Import and initialize Echo in resources/js/app.js
Add Vite env variables for Reverb connection
Create useEcho() composable for component usage
Test private channel subscription
Implement reconnection logic for dropped connections
Add connection status indicator to UI (optional)
items.verification: Open browser console; verify Echo connects; subscribe to test channel

items.title: Create Private Channel Authorization
items.description: Set up private channels for landlord and tenant dashboards. Each user should only receive broadcasts for their own data.
steps: Create channel authorization in routes/channels.php
Define landlord.{landlordId} private channel
Define tenant.{tenantId} private channel
Define lease.{leaseId} private channel for shared events
Implement authorization callbacks with proper guards
Test unauthorized access is rejected
Add logging for channel subscription attempts

channels.landlord.{landlordId}: User is the landlord or caretaker of landlord
channels.lease.{leaseId}: User is landlord/caretaker OR tenant of this lease

items.verification: Subscribe to channel as authorized user (success); subscribe as wrong user (failure)

items.title: Create PaymentReceived Broadcast Event
items.description: Broadcast payment confirmations to both landlord and tenant dashboards in real-time. This is the highest-value real-time event.
steps: Create PaymentReceived event class implementing ShouldBroadcast
Define broadcastOn() to send to both landlord and tenant channels
Include payment details, updated balance, and invoice status
Dispatch event from PaymentController::recordPayment()
Dispatch event from M-Pesa webhook on successful payment
Dispatch event from Paystack webhook on successful payment
Add WhatsApp notification trigger from same event
Create frontend handler in Dashboard.vue and TenantFinances.vue
items.verification: Record payment; verify real-time update on both dashboards without page refresh

items.title: Replace M-Pesa Polling with Real-time Updates
items.description: The TenantFinances/Pay.vue currently polls for M-Pesa STK Push status. Replace with WebSocket listener for instant feedback.
steps: Identify current polling logic in Pay.vue (setInterval for status check)
Create MpesaPaymentStatusChanged event
Dispatch event from MpesaWebhookController::stkCallback()
Add Echo listener in Pay.vue for payment status
Show real-time status updates: processing → success/failed
Remove polling interval after WebSocket connection established
Add fallback polling for WebSocket connection failures
Implement optimistic UI update before server confirmation
items.verification: Initiate M-Pesa payment; verify instant UI update on callback (no 3-second delay)

items.title: Real-time Notification Badge Updates
items.description: Update the notification bell badge count in real-time when new notifications arrive, without requiring page refresh.
steps: Create NewNotification event for badge updates
Dispatch event from NotificationService::send() after in_app delivery
Add Echo listener in NotificationBell.vue
Increment badge count on new notification
Play notification sound (optional, respect user preference)
Show toast/snackbar for high-priority notifications
Update navBadges in Inertia shared data
items.verification: Send notification to user; verify badge updates instantly without refresh

items.title: Implement Urgency-Based Channel Selection
items.description: Different notification types have different urgency levels. Critical notifications should use more aggressive channels.
steps: Define urgency levels: critical, urgent, important, informational
Map notification types to urgency levels
Implement getChannelsForUrgency() method in NotificationService
Critical: WhatsApp + SMS + Push + In-app (all channels)
Urgent: WhatsApp + Push + In-app
Important: WhatsApp + Email + In-app
Informational: Email + In-app only
Add urgency field to notifications table for tracking
items.verification: Send critical notification; verify all channels attempted

items.title: Implement Quiet Hours Respect
items.description: Respect tenant quiet hours preferences. Non-urgent notifications should be queued for delivery after quiet hours end.
steps: Audit current quiet hours implementation in NotificationService
Ensure quiet hours check happens before send, not after queue
Queue non-urgent notifications for quiet_hours_end time
Allow critical/urgent notifications to bypass quiet hours
Store tenant timezone for accurate quiet hours calculation
Add 'sent during quiet hours' flag for compliance tracking
Update preference UI to clearly show quiet hours impact
items.verification: Set quiet hours 22:00-08:00; send informational notification at 23:00; verify queued for 08:00

items.title: Implement WhatsApp Inbound Message Webhook
items.description: Handle incoming WhatsApp messages from tenants. Capture responses and create actionable records.
steps: Create inbound webhook endpoint: POST /webhooks/whatsapp/inbound
Parse Twilio inbound message payload
Match sender phone to tenant user record
Determine context (reply to notification vs new message)
Create TenantMessage model to store inbound messages
Link message to original notification if reply
Notify landlord/caretaker of new tenant message
Auto-create support ticket for certain keywords
items.verification: Reply to WhatsApp notification; verify message captured in TenantMessage table

items.title: Create Landlord Inbox for Tenant Messages
items.description: Landlords need a unified inbox to see all tenant messages across WhatsApp, SMS, and email responses.
steps: Create Inbox page component: resources/js/Pages/Inbox/Index.vue
Add route: GET /inbox
Create InboxController with message listing and reply functionality
Show messages grouped by tenant
Display message thread with original notification context
Allow landlord to reply (sends via same channel tenant used)
Mark messages as read/unread
Add inbox badge to navigation
items.verification: Receive tenant WhatsApp reply; see in inbox; reply; verify tenant receives response

items.title: Auto-Create Support Tickets from Tenant Messages
items.description: When tenants send messages containing issue keywords, automatically create support tickets with context.
steps: Define keyword patterns for ticket creation: 'problem', 'broken', 'issue', 'repair', etc.
Create TenantMessageAnalyzer service for keyword detection
Auto-create Ticket when keywords detected
Attach original message and notification context to ticket
Notify caretaker of new ticket via push/WhatsApp
Send confirmation to tenant that ticket was created
Add ticket link to landlord inbox message view
items.verification: Send WhatsApp message with 'broken pipe'; verify Ticket created automatically

items.title: Real-time Landlord Dashboard Metrics Update
items.description: When payments are received, update landlord dashboard financial metrics in real-time without page refresh.
steps: Identify metrics affected by payments: Monthly Revenue, Collection Rate, Arrears
Add metrics calculation to PaymentReceived event payload
Create Echo listener in Dashboard.vue for payment events
Implement incremental metric updates (add to current, don't refetch)
Show visual indicator when metrics update (subtle animation)
Update recentPayments list with new payment
Update unit status if payment clears arrears
items.verification: Record payment; verify dashboard metrics update instantly

items.title: Real-time Ticket Status Synchronization
items.description: When ticket status changes, update both landlord dashboard (action items) and tenant dashboard (my tickets) in real-time.
steps: Create TicketStatusChanged event implementing ShouldBroadcast
Dispatch from TicketController::update() on status change
Broadcast to landlord.{landlordId} and tenant.{tenantId} channels
Add Echo listener in landlord Dashboard.vue for ticket updates
Add Echo listener in tenant Dashboard.vue for ticket updates
Update action items count on landlord dashboard
Update ticket status in tenant 'My Tickets' widget
Send WhatsApp notification to tenant on status change
items.verification: Change ticket status; verify both dashboards update without refresh

items.title: Real-time Invitation Status Updates
items.description: When a caretaker or tenant accepts an invitation, notify the landlord dashboard in real-time.
steps: Create InvitationAccepted event implementing ShouldBroadcast
Dispatch from InvitationController::accept()
Broadcast to landlord.{landlordId} channel
Add Echo listener in relevant landlord pages
Update invitation list status without refresh
Show toast notification with acceptance details
Update caretaker/tenant count in navigation badges
items.verification: Accept invitation; verify landlord sees status change instantly

items.title: Implement Connection Status Indicator
items.description: Show users when their WebSocket connection is active, reconnecting, or disconnected. Provide fallback behavior.
steps: Create ConnectionStatus.vue component
Listen to Echo connection state changes
Show subtle indicator in header/footer
Implement automatic reconnection with exponential backoff
Switch to polling fallback when WebSocket unavailable for 30+ seconds
Log connection issues for debugging
Track connection quality metrics

connectionStates.disconnected: Red dot, 'Offline - updates may be delayed'

items.verification: Disconnect network; verify indicator shows offline; reconnect; verify indicator shows online

items.title: Implement WhatsApp Payment Link Generation
items.description: When sending rent reminders or arrears notices via WhatsApp, include a clickable payment link that deep-links to the payment page.
steps: Generate signed URLs for tenant payment pages
Add payment_link variable to WhatsApp templates
Include M-Pesa paybill details in template
Track payment link clicks (via redirect route)
Expire payment links after invoice is paid
Add UTM parameters for analytics tracking
items.verification: Click payment link in WhatsApp; verify redirects to payment page with correct invoice

progressTracking.description: Track progress in progress.txt file, committed to repo after each task

format.blockers: Any blockers or notes for next iteration

metrics: WhatsApp delivery rate > 95% for opted-in tenants
Fallback to SMS triggers within 1 hour of WhatsApp failure
Quiet hours compliance: 0 non-urgent notifications during quiet hours
Two-way message capture rate: 100% of inbound WhatsApp messages logged
Reverb WebSocket server stable under 1000 concurrent connections
